summary: Diagnose and fix React re-render and bundle-size problems.
content:
---
name: react-performance
description: Use when a React app feels slow, re-renders too much, or ships a large bundle. Covers memoization, list virtualization, code-splitting, and profiling.
---

# React performance

When the UI feels slow:
1. Profile first with the React DevTools Profiler; find the components that re-render most.
2. Stabilize props: wrap callbacks in useCallback and derived objects in useMemo only where a memoized child depends on them.
3. Memoize expensive children with React.memo; give lists stable keys.
4. Virtualize long lists (react-window / virtualized) instead of rendering thousands of nodes.
5. Code-split routes and heavy components with dynamic import / React.lazy.
6. Never optimize blindly — measure before and after.

summary: Build with the Next.js App Router: server components, data fetching, caching.
content:
---
name: nextjs-app-router
description: Use when building or debugging a Next.js App Router app — server vs client components, data fetching, route handlers, and caching.
---

# Next.js App Router

- Default to Server Components; add 'use client' only when you need state, effects, or browser APIs.
- Fetch data in Server Components with async/await; colocate it with the route that needs it.
- Use route handlers (app/api/*/route.ts) for backend logic; they run on the server.
- Control caching explicitly with fetch cache options and revalidate; do not assume defaults.
- Keep client bundles small: pass server data down as props rather than fetching on the client.

summary: Add and compose shadcn/ui components correctly with Tailwind.
content:
---
name: shadcn-ui
description: Use when adding or composing shadcn/ui components in a React/Tailwind app. Covers the CLI, component ownership, and theming.
---

# shadcn/ui

- shadcn/ui is copy-in, not a dependency: `npx shadcn@latest add <component>` writes the component into your repo, so you own and can edit it.
- Compose primitives (Button, Dialog, Command) rather than rebuilding them.
- Theme with CSS variables in globals.css; keep tokens consistent across light and dark.
- Prefer the cn() helper for conditional class names.

summary: Write maintainable Tailwind: tokens, responsive design, dark mode.
content:
---
name: tailwind-styling
description: Use when styling with Tailwind CSS — design tokens, responsive breakpoints, dark mode, and avoiding class soup.
---

# Tailwind styling

- Drive spacing/color from the theme; avoid arbitrary values ([13px]) unless necessary.
- Extract repeated class strings into components, not @apply soup.
- Mobile-first: base styles then sm: md: lg: overrides.
- Support dark mode with the dark: variant and a single token set.

summary: Make front-end UIs accessible: semantics, keyboard, ARIA, contrast.
content:
---
name: web-accessibility
description: Use when building UI that must be accessible — semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, ARIA, focus management, and color contrast.
---

# Web accessibility

- Use semantic elements (button, nav, main, label) before reaching for ARIA.
- Everything interactive must be keyboard-reachable and have a visible focus ring.
- Associate labels with inputs; give icons accessible names.
- Manage focus on route changes and when opening dialogs; trap focus in modals.
- Check contrast (>= 4.5:1 for text) and don't rely on color alone.

summary: Author reliable Playwright end-to-end tests with role-based locators.
content:
---
name: playwright-testing
description: Use when writing or fixing Playwright end-to-end tests. Covers role-based locators, auto-waiting, and avoiding flakiness.
---

# Playwright testing

- Prefer role-based locators: getByRole, getByLabel, getByText over CSS selectors.
- Rely on Playwright's auto-waiting; avoid manual sleeps.
- Keep tests isolated: fresh context per test; seed state via API, not the UI, when possible.
- Use expect() web-first assertions so retries handle async UI.
- Trace failures with --trace on.

summary: Build well-typed FastAPI endpoints with Pydantic and dependency injection.
content:
---
name: fastapi-endpoints
description: Use when adding FastAPI routes — Pydantic models, dependency injection, validation, and error handling.
---

# FastAPI endpoints

- Define request/response with Pydantic models; let FastAPI validate and document them.
- Use Depends() for shared concerns (auth, db sessions) instead of globals.
- Return explicit status codes; raise HTTPException for errors.
- Keep route functions thin; put logic in services.

summary: Idiomatic Go error handling: wrapping, sentinel errors, and errors.Is/As.
content:
---
name: go-error-handling
description: Use when writing or reviewing Go error handling — wrapping with %w, sentinel errors, errors.Is/As, and when to panic.
---

# Go error handling

- Return errors, don't panic (except truly unrecoverable startup failures).
- Wrap with fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err) so callers can errors.Is/As.
- Define sentinel errors (var ErrNotFound = errors.New(...)) for conditions callers branch on.
- Handle each error once; don't log and return the same error at every layer.
